description: Enterprise-ready, framework-agnostic validation library. Rules are plain JSON data. Supports multiple adapters (default, Zod v4). Works in Vue 2, Vue 3, Node.js. Full i18n support with code+params system.

concepts.name: schema
concepts.description: A plain JavaScript object (JSON-serialisable) that describes how a value should look. Contains type, rules[], nullable, optional, label, shape (for objects), items (for arrays).

concepts.name: rule
concepts.description: A constraint definition object: { type: string, value?: any }. The 'type' maps to a registered rule function. The 'value' is the rule parameter (e.g. minimum length).

concepts.name: adapter
concepts.description: An object with a validate(schema, value, options) method that returns an array of raw error objects { field, code, params }. The library ships with 'default' (no deps) and 'zod' (Zod v4).
concepts.contract: { validate(schema, value, options): Array<{ field, code, params }> }

concepts.name: validation_result
concepts.description: The object returned by validate(). Always contains valid (boolean) and errors (array). Never throws.

shape.errors: Array<{ field: string, code: string, params: object, message: string }>

concepts.name: error_format
concepts.description: Standard error object shape used throughout the library.

shape.code: Error code used for i18n lookup (e.g. 'minLength', 'email', 'required')
shape.params: Interpolation parameters for the message template (e.g. { min: 5, actual: 2 })

concepts.name: i18n
concepts.description: Localisation system based on code+params with no hardcoded messages. Supports runtime locale registration and per-call locale override. Fallback chain: requested → 'en' → code itself.

api.name: validate
api.description: Main validation function. Never throws. Orchestrates buildSchema → adapter.validate → normalizeErrors → formatResult.

params.options.locale: Override locale for this call only
params.options.adapter: Override adapter for this call only (must be registered)
params.options.field: Root field name for error paths

api.returns: { valid: boolean, errors: Array<{ field, code, params, message }> }

api.name: registerRule
api.description: Register a custom validation rule globally.

params.name: Unique rule identifier string
params.fn: Rule function: (value, params) => true | { code, params }

params.name: Unique adapter identifier string
params.adapter: Object with validate(schema, value, options) method returning array of raw errors

api.name: setAdapter
api.description: Set the globally active adapter. Pass 'default' to revert to built-in engine.

api.name: buildSchema
api.description: Parse and normalise a schema definition. Results are cached by JSON fingerprint.
api.example: const schema = buildSchema({ type: 'string', rules: [{ type: 'email' }] })

api.name: setLocale
api.description: Set the globally active locale for all translations.

api.name: getLocale
api.description: Returns the currently active locale string.

api.name: translate
api.description: Translate an error code to a human-readable message. Interpolates {key} placeholders with params values.
api.example: translate('minLength', { min: 5, actual: 2 }) // → 'Minimum length is 5 characters, got 2'

api.name: registerLocale
api.description: Register or merge messages into a locale at runtime.

api.name: clearCache
api.description: Clear the internal schema definition cache. Useful after hot-reloading schemas.

rules.name: required
rules.description: Value must not be null, undefined or empty string

rules.name: type
rules.description: Value must match the expected JS type

params.value: string — one of: string, number, boolean, array, object, function

rules.name: oneOf
rules.description: Value must be one of the listed values (strict equality)

rules.name: custom
rules.description: Inline validation function. fn(value, params) must return true/undefined (pass), false (fail), string (fail with message), or { code, params } (fail with explicit error)

rules.name: minLength
rules.description: String must have at least N characters

rules.name: maxLength
rules.description: String must have at most N characters

rules.name: email
rules.description: String must be a valid email address

rules.name: url
rules.description: String must be a valid HTTP/HTTPS URL

rules.name: pattern
rules.description: String must match the given RegExp or pattern string

rules.name: notEmpty
rules.description: String must not be blank (trimmed length > 0)

rules.name: min
rules.description: Number must be >= value

rules.name: max
rules.description: Number must be <= value

rules.name: integer
rules.description: Number must be a whole integer (no decimals)

rules.name: positive
rules.description: Number must be strictly greater than 0

rules.name: negative
rules.description: Number must be strictly less than 0

rules.name: minItems
rules.description: Array must have at least N elements

rules.name: maxItems
rules.description: Array must have at most N elements

rules.name: noEmpty
rules.description: Array must have at least one element

rules.name: unique
rules.description: All array elements must be distinct (deep equality via JSON)

rules.name: requiredKeys
rules.description: Object must contain all specified keys with non-null/undefined values

params.value: string[] — array of required key names

adapters.name: default
adapters.description: Built-in rule-based adapter. Zero external dependencies. Used automatically when no adapter is set.

adapters.name: zod
adapters.description: Zod v4 adapter. Translates schema definitions to Zod schemas, runs safeParse(), maps issues to library error format.

i18n.description: All error messages use a code+params system. No hardcoded strings. Fallback chain: requested locale → 'en' → raw code.
i18n.message_format: Templates use {key} placeholders: 'Minimum length is {min} characters, got {actual}'

examples.description: Validate a string with email rule

examples.code: validate({ type: 'object', shape: { name: { type: 'string', rules: [{ type: 'minLength', value: 2 }] }, email: { type: 'string', rules: [{ type: 'email' }] } } }, { name: 'Alice', email: 'alice@example.com' })

examples.description: Validate an array with item schema
examples.code: validate({ type: 'array', rules: [{ type: 'minItems', value: 1 }], items: { type: 'string', rules: [{ type: 'email' }] } }, ['bad-email'])

errors.message: Must be a valid email address

examples.description: Change locale and get translated message

errors.message: Deve ser um endereço de e-mail válido

examples.code: const schema = await fetch('/api/schema').then(r => r.json()); validate(schema, value)
examples.note: Schema is a plain object — fetch result works directly

examples.description: Register and use Zod v4 adapter
examples.code: const zodAdapter = require('validators/src/adapters/zod'); registerAdapter('zod', zodAdapter); validate(schema, value, { adapter: 'zod' })
validate({ type: 'string', rules: [{ type: 'custom', fn: (v) => v.startsWith('BR') || 'Must start with BR' }] }, 'US-123')
validate({ type: 'string', nullable: true, rules: [{ type: 'email' }] }, null)

extension_points.custom_rule: registerRule(name, fn) — fn: (value, params) => true | { code, params }

security.note: validate() wraps all execution in try/catch and always returns { valid, errors }

mcp_intent: This file enables AI assistants and LLMs to understand how to use, extend and reason about this library. It contains the full API surface, all rules with parameters and examples, i18n instructions and extension patterns.
